meta.title: Japan-Readiness Index
meta.subtitle: How ready are foreign developer tools for the Japanese market?
meta.author: greymoth
meta.method_note: Every signal below was checked live against each company's own public marketing site on 2026-06-25 using direct HTTP requests (status codes, HTML lang, hreflang) and rendered-page reads (Japanese copy, pricing currency). No estimates. Scores are reproducible from the evidence column. Sites are the property of their respective owners; this report quotes only publicly observable facts.

signals.label: 特商法 page (Specified Commercial Transactions Act)
signals.why: Legally expected for any paid online service sold in Japan. Its absence is a hard stop for many corporate procurement / legal teams — the deal dies before engineering is ever evaluated.

signals.label: Localized Japanese site / /ja route
signals.why: A real Japanese-language entry point. Scored on rendered Japanese marketing copy, not on a route merely existing — an English SPA shell served at /ja scores zero.

signals.label: Japanese content / language switcher
signals.why: Any genuine Japanese copy or a switcher that offers Japanese. Partial credit for token Japanese; zero for none.

signals.label: hreflang="ja" alternate
signals.why: Without it, Japanese-language search traffic never lands on a localized page — invisible in JP Google results.

signals.label: JPY pricing / currency
signals.why: USD-only forces JP finance to FX-convert and absorb forex fees, and gives them no clean number to approve. A JPY display toggle is enough; billing can stay USD.

companies.name: Auth0

tokushoho.evidence: Footer of auth0.com/jp shows Legal / Privacy / Terms only — no 特商法 page.

ja_site.evidence: auth0.com/jp = HTTP 200, <html lang="ja">, fully Japanese marketing copy (heading 「Auth0：誰もが安全にアクセスするために。」, nav 開発者/製品/料金体系).

jp_content.evidence: Entire /jp site is native Japanese, including pricing page in Japanese.

jpy_pricing.evidence: auth0.com/jp/pricing is in Japanese but priced in USD: 「$35 / 月」, 「$385」. No JPY, no toggle.

companies.gap: Has the strongest JP localization in this index (full Japanese site, JP search-discoverable) — but still prices in USD and has no 特商法 page, the two procurement-gate items.
companies.fix: Add a 特商法に基づく表記 page to the /jp footer and a JPY display reference on the JP pricing page. Two static changes finish an otherwise JP-ready front door.

companies.name: Twilio Segment

tokushoho.evidence: twilio.com/ja-jp footer = privacy / security / terms / careers — no 特商法 link.

ja_site.evidence: twilio.com/ja-jp = <html lang="ja-jp">, native Japanese marketing (heading 「驚くような顧客体験が生まれる場所」), Segment covered (「Twilio Segment 顧客データプラットフォーム」).

jp_content.evidence: Full Japanese engagement-platform copy across the /ja-jp site.

hreflang_ja.evidence: /ja redirects to /ja-jp; Twilio emits multi-locale hreflang.

jpy_pricing.evidence: Segment pricing is USD-only with no JPY display.

companies.gap: Segment rides Twilio's mature ja-jp site, so localization and JP discoverability are strong — but no 特商法 page and USD-only pricing remain.
companies.fix: Add 特商法 to the JP footer and a JPY pricing reference for the Segment line.

companies.name: HashiCorp (Terraform / Vault)

tokushoho.evidence: Footer of the JP site lists Terms / Trust Center / Trademark / Trade Controls — no 特商法 page.

ja_site.evidence: hashicorp.com/ja = <html lang="ja">, full Japanese marketing copy (heading 「HashiCorp: イノベーションはインフラストラクチャからはじまります」, nav プラットフォーム/製品/開発者/会社情報).

jp_content.evidence: Extensive native Japanese product descriptions, case studies, webinars.

hreflang_ja.evidence: Homepage hreflang not confirmed (bot rate-limit 429 on direct route probes); JP site itself renders lang=ja.

jpy_pricing.evidence: Cloud pricing is USD-denominated; no JPY toggle observed.

companies.gap: A genuinely localized Japanese site — but missing the 特商法 page and JPY pricing that JP procurement and finance look for.
companies.fix: Publish a 特商法 page in the JP footer; add JPY reference pricing. The localization is already done — the commercial/legal surface lags it.

companies.name: Appwrite

tokushoho.evidence: No 特商法 page on appwrite.io.

ja_site.evidence: appwrite.io English-only, no /ja route, no language switcher.

jp_content.evidence: Product ships a bundled Japanese UI locale: app/config/locale/translations/ja.json exists — but at 247 keys vs en 295 it is ~16% (48 keys) behind (gh raw-file count).

hreflang_ja.evidence: No hreflang ja in homepage markup.

companies.gap: The product UI has a Japanese locale — but it is measurably 16% behind English, and the marketing/commercial surface (site, pricing, 特商法) is fully English. The clearest 'quantifiable gap' case in this index.
companies.fix: Fill the 48 missing ja.json keys, then add a JP landing page + hreflang + 特商法 so JP buyers can find and approve it.

companies.name: Coolify

tokushoho.evidence: No 特商法 page on coolify.io.

jp_content.evidence: Product ships a maintained Japanese UI (lang/ja.json), a peer of en.

companies.gap: Self-host dashboard is localized to Japanese, but the marketing site offers nothing for a Japanese buyer.
companies.fix: A /ja landing page + hreflang + 特商法 page to match the already-localized product.

companies.name: Formbricks

jp_content.evidence: Product ships a Japanese UI (apps/web/locales/ja-JP.json), v4.0 ja support announced.

companies.gap: In-product Japanese exists, but the commercial site is English-only with no 特商法 and no JP search presence.
companies.fix: JP landing page + hreflang + 特商法 page.

companies.name: Logto

tokushoho.evidence: No 特商法 page; USD pricing on logto.io.

jp_content.evidence: Product ships a bundled ja UI locale (packages/phrases/src/locales/ja), a peer of en — but the marketing site has zero Japanese.

companies.gap: Solid in-product Japanese UI, but the buying surface (site, pricing, legal) is entirely English — JP buyers never see the localized product before procurement stalls.
companies.fix: Stand up a /ja landing page + hreflang + 特商法; the product is already JP-capable, only the front door isn't.

companies.name: ToolJet

jp_content.evidence: Product UI can now display Japanese via a community ja.json PR (all 822 keys), but the marketing site has no Japanese.

jpy_pricing.evidence: USD only: $0 / $79 / $199 per builder/mo; Enterprise from $3,000/mo; no ¥.

companies.gap: The builder UI is now Japanese-capable, but the commercial surface is English-only with no JPY and no 特商法 — JP buyers still stall before procurement.
companies.fix: A /ja landing page + hreflang + 特商法 + JPY reference to match the now-localizable product.

companies.name: Umami

jp_content.evidence: Product ships and maintains a Japanese UI (public/intl/language/ja-JP.json).

companies.gap: Dashboard is fully Japanese-capable and actively maintained, yet the site is English-only with no JP discoverability.
companies.fix: Localized landing page + hreflang + 特商法 to surface the JP-ready product to JP search.

companies.name: Algolia

jp_content.evidence: Language switcher offers English/Deutsch/français/Brazilian Portuguese/Spanish/Italian — Japanese is NOT offered.

hreflang_ja.evidence: 3 hreflang tags present but none is ja (EN/DE/FR confirmed, no ja).

companies.gap: Notably, Algolia DID localize — to European languages and Brazilian Portuguese — but deliberately skipped Japanese. The infrastructure exists; Japan was simply not prioritized.
companies.fix: Add ja to the existing language switcher + hreflang ja + a JP 特商法 page + JPY. The hardest part (i18n infra) is already built.

companies.name: Appsmith

tokushoho.evidence: No 特商法 notice on the site.

jp_content.evidence: No Japanese content; the editor has no i18n layer (1,310 hardcoded English TS string constants, no i18n library in client deps).

companies.gap: Zero JP localization at both layers, and the editor lacks any i18n foundation (open issue #4404 since 2021, de-prioritized). JP-readiness here is an i18n-infra + GTM project, not a drop-in fix.
companies.fix: GTM surface (特商法 + /ja + hreflang + JPY) is the quick layer; product Japanese requires building the i18n foundation first.

companies.name: BrowserStack

ja_site.evidence: browserstack.com/ja and /guide/ja both HTTP 404; <html lang="en-US">; no clean /ja site.

jp_content.evidence: No localized Japanese marketing site.

companies.gap: No clean Japanese site path; the front door is English/USD with no 特商法.
companies.fix: 特商法 + a real /ja site + hreflang + JPY.

companies.name: Clerk

tokushoho.evidence: No 特商法 page; footer = Pricing/Docs/Blog/Changelog.

jp_content.evidence: No Japanese text on the site.

jpy_pricing.evidence: USD only: Free, $25/mo Pro, $300/mo Business, etc.; no ¥.

companies.gap: Popular auth choice for JP indie/startup stacks, but the entire surface is English/USD with no JP entry point.
companies.fix: 特商法 page + /ja landing + hreflang + JPY reference; a bundled ja for the prebuilt auth components would also help.

companies.name: Contentful

jp_content.evidence: No Japanese content.

companies.gap: English/USD-only; no JP entry point.
companies.fix: 特商法 + /ja + hreflang + JPY.

companies.name: Directus

jp_content.evidence: No Japanese content on the marketing site.

companies.gap: English/USD-only marketing surface; no JP entry point.
companies.fix: 特商法 + /ja + hreflang + JPY.

companies.name: Doppler

jp_content.evidence: No Japanese content.

companies.gap: English/USD-only; no JP entry point. As with Infisical, JP copy for a secrets tool needs security-literate native review.
companies.fix: 特商法 + /ja + hreflang + JPY.

companies.name: Flagsmith

ja_site.evidence: <html lang="en">; no /ja path or switcher in markup.

jp_content.evidence: No Japanese content; no i18n layer in the React frontend (no translation lib, hardcoded English JSX across ~160 of 671 components).

companies.gap: Same as Unleash — real JP feature-flag demand, but no product i18n and no JP marketing surface.
companies.fix: Product i18n foundation + ja; site 特商法 + /ja + hreflang + JPY.

companies.name: Fly.io

jp_content.evidence: No Japanese content.

companies.gap: English/USD-only; no JP entry point.
companies.fix: 特商法 + /ja + hreflang + JPY.

companies.name: Hasura

jp_content.evidence: No Japanese content.

companies.gap: English/USD-only; no JP entry point.
companies.fix: 特商法 + /ja + hreflang + JPY.

companies.name: Infisical

tokushoho.evidence: Footer = Terms/Privacy/Subprocessors/SLA — no 特商法 page.

ja_site.evidence: infisical.com/ja returns HTTP 404; docs English-only; no switcher.

jp_content.evidence: No Japanese text on the site.

hreflang_ja.evidence: No hreflang on top page or docs.

companies.gap: Has JP developer awareness (Qiita/Zenn writeups) but no localized surface. For a secrets tool, future JP copy must be security-literate native review, not raw MT — a mistranslated scope/rotation instruction is a security incident.
companies.fix: A /legal/tokushoho page, a /ja landing + top-10 docs, hreflang ja, and a JPY reference price.

companies.name: Inngest

ja_site.evidence: inngest.com/ja and /ja/docs both HTTP 404; docs English-only, no switcher.

jp_content.evidence: No Japanese copy on the site.

jpy_pricing.evidence: USD: Hobby $0/mo, Pro from $75/mo; no JPY; no 消費税 line.

companies.gap: Durable-execution is conceptual — adoption hinges on docs readability. English-only docs leak the very JP evaluators Inngest already attracts on Zenn/Qiita; the dev gets interested, then loses the thread translating concurrency semantics under deadline.
companies.fix: 特商法 page, /ja landing + Quick Start, hreflang, JPY + 消費税 line, and translate the 5-10 highest-traffic docs pages first.

companies.name: Linear

ja_site.evidence: linear.app/ja = HTTP 200 but renders an English 'Loading…' SPA shell (0 Japanese chars) — not a localized page.

jp_content.evidence: No rendered Japanese marketing copy.

companies.gap: Like Vercel, /ja is an English SPA shell, not real localization. The front door is closed despite the route returning 200.
companies.fix: A genuinely localized /ja landing + hreflang + 特商法 + JPY.

companies.name: Mux

jp_content.evidence: No Japanese content.

companies.gap: English/USD-only; no JP entry point.
companies.fix: 特商法 + /ja + hreflang + JPY.

companies.name: n8n

ja_site.evidence: n8n.io/ja 301-redirects to /ja/ which returns HTTP 404; <html lang="en">.

jp_content.evidence: No Japanese content.

companies.gap: A /ja redirect exists but lands on 404 — no real localization. English-only surface.
companies.fix: 特商法 + a real /ja landing + hreflang + JPY.

companies.name: Neon

jp_content.evidence: No Japanese content or switcher.

companies.gap: Serverless Postgres is a natural fit for JP SaaS, but the site is fully English/USD with no JP route.
companies.fix: 特商法 + /ja landing + hreflang + JPY toggle.

companies.name: Novu

jp_content.evidence: Dashboard/web app UI has no ja locale (content i18n is a feature, but the admin UI is English).

companies.gap: High-profile (39k+ stars) but the management UI and the whole marketing surface are English-only.
companies.fix: Dashboard ja locale; 特商法 + /ja + hreflang + JPY for the site.

companies.name: PlanetScale

jp_content.evidence: No Japanese content or switcher.

companies.gap: No Japanese surface at all; a JP buyer cannot evaluate or approve it without FX guesswork.
companies.fix: 特商法 + /ja + hreflang + JPY.

companies.name: PostHog

ja_site.evidence: posthog.com/ja = HTTP 404; no <html lang> found; no switcher.

jp_content.evidence: No Japanese content.

jpy_pricing.evidence: States 'All prices are in US Dollars (USD), excluding taxes.' No ¥.

companies.gap: Explicitly USD-only and English-only; no JP surface.
companies.fix: 特商法 + /ja + hreflang + JPY.

companies.name: Railway

jp_content.evidence: No Japanese content.

companies.gap: Popular with JP indie devs, but no localized surface and no 特商法.
companies.fix: 特商法 + /ja + hreflang + JPY.

companies.name: Render

jp_content.evidence: No Japanese content.

companies.gap: English/USD-only; no JP entry point.
companies.fix: 特商法 + /ja + hreflang + JPY.

companies.name: Retool

jp_content.evidence: No Japanese content.

companies.gap: Strong internal-tools demand in JP enterprise, but English/USD-only with no 特商法.
companies.fix: 特商法 + /ja + hreflang + JPY.

companies.name: Sanity

jp_content.evidence: No Japanese content.

companies.gap: English/USD-only; no JP entry point.
companies.fix: 特商法 + /ja + hreflang + JPY.

companies.name: Sentry

tokushoho.evidence: No 特商法 link on the pricing page or footer.

ja_site.evidence: sentry.io/ja redirects to /auth/login/ja/ (an English login page with a ja locale param) — no Japanese marketing site.

jp_content.evidence: No Japanese marketing copy; the /ja redirect lands on an English login (lang=en, 0 JP chars).

hreflang_ja.evidence: No hreflang ja in homepage HTML.

jpy_pricing.evidence: USD only: $0, $26/mo, $80/mo; no ¥; no currency switcher.

companies.gap: The /ja route is only a login-form locale, not a localized site. JP buyers see English/USD throughout with no 特商法.
companies.fix: 特商法 + a real /ja marketing landing + hreflang + JPY.

companies.name: Supabase

jp_content.evidence: No Japanese content on the marketing site.

companies.gap: Huge JP developer mindshare, zero localized front door — JP traffic lands on an English/USD page with no 特商法.
companies.fix: 特商法 + /ja landing + hreflang + JPY; docs translation for the highest-traffic guides.

companies.name: SuperTokens

jp_content.evidence: Auth UI has an i18n hook (translationFunc) but ships no bundled ja translation (only an example).

companies.gap: Auth UI in Japanese is often mandatory for JP adoption, yet no bundled ja locale ships and the site is English-only.
companies.fix: Ship a bundled ja login-UI locale; 特商法 + /ja + hreflang + JPY for the site.

companies.name: Turso

tokushoho.evidence: Footer lists Terms/Privacy/Trust/About/Contact/Support — no 特商法; /terms-of-use = CHISELSTRIKE INC., Delaware.

ja_site.evidence: turso.tech/ja and docs.turso.tech/ja both return HTTP 404; no language switcher.

jp_content.evidence: No Japanese text anywhere on site or docs.

jpy_pricing.evidence: USD only: $0, $4.99/mo, $24.92/mo, $416.58/mo, $0.75/GB. No JPY, no toggle.

companies.gap: Strong organic pull with Japanese indie/SaaS devs (libSQL/edge-SQLite), but every JP-facing surface is missing — the front door is fully closed at the procurement and finance gate.
companies.fix: One static 特商法 page + a /ja landing + translated quickstart + hreflang + a JPY display toggle. All footer/page-level, no product change.

companies.name: Unleash

jp_content.evidence: Dashboard English-only; no UI i18n framework (only date-fns date locales).

companies.gap: Feature-flag demand is solid among JP SaaS/enterprise, but the product is English-only and the site has zero JP presence.
companies.fix: Add a UI i18n layer + ja for the product; 特商法 + /ja + hreflang + JPY for the site.

companies.name: Upstash

jp_content.evidence: No Japanese content.

companies.gap: English/USD-only; no JP entry point.
companies.fix: 特商法 + /ja + hreflang + JPY.

companies.name: Vercel

ja_site.evidence: vercel.com/ja = HTTP 200 but is an English dashboard SPA shell (<html lang="en-US">, 0 Japanese chars) — a locale param, not a localized site.

jp_content.evidence: No rendered Japanese marketing copy.

companies.gap: The /ja route exists but serves English — a textbook 'looks localized, isn't' case. No real Japanese marketing site, no 特商法, no JPY.
companies.fix: Turn /ja into a real localized landing page (not an English shell) + hreflang + 特商法 + JPY.

companies.name: Windmill

jp_content.evidence: Product UI (Svelte) has no i18n layer; English-only.

companies.gap: Workflow/automation has JP developer demand, but neither product nor site offers Japanese.
companies.fix: UI i18n foundation + ja; site 特商法 + /ja + hreflang + JPY.
